connection.note: extract-based - original logical source was 'excel'; actual rows come from the packaged .hyper file, not a live connection

fields.caption: 2012 sales
fields.tableau_formula:
if year([Order Date])=2012 then [Sales]
else NULL end

fields.caption: 2013 sales
fields.tableau_formula:
if year([Order Date])=2013 then [Sales]
else NULL end

connection.note: extract-based - original logical source was 'excel'; actual rows come from the packaged .hyper file, not a live connection

fields.caption: Years until conversion
fields.tableau_formula:
If isnull([Date Super Center]) then NULL
else DATEDIFF('year',[First Opened],[Date Super Center] ) end

fields.caption: Converted?
fields.tableau_formula:
If isnull([Date Super Center]) then " "
elseif [First Opened]=[Date Super Center] then "Opened as SuperCenter"
else "Converted " + STR(DATEDIFF('year',[First Opened],[Date Super Center] )) + " years later" end

connection.note: extract-based - original logical source was 'excel'; actual rows come from the packaged .hyper file, not a live connection

fields.caption: Achieved Quota
fields.tableau_formula:
if SUM([Achievement (copy)]) >= AVG([Parameters].[New Quota]) then "100% +"
elseif SUM([Achievement (copy)]) >= 0.75*AVG([Parameters].[New Quota]) then "75-100%"
elseif SUM([Achievement (copy)]) >= 0.5*AVG([Parameters].[New Quota]) then "50-75%"
else "Below 50%" end

fields.caption: Sort by field
fields.tableau_formula:
if [Parameters].[Parameter 1]="Names" then 0
elseif [Parameters].[Parameter 1]="% quota descending" then -[Calculation_0440925131659539]
else [Calculation_0440925131659539] end

worksheets.name: Sales Planning
worksheets.title_text: Estimated Quota Attainment Results with These Assumptions

theme.source_note: Tableau has no single global theme file - aggregated from per-worksheet mark-color formats; treat as a starting palette, not an authoritative theme to clone.

limitations_encountered.issue: extract-based (.hyper) data source - row data requires a separate extraction step (tableauhyperapi -> Parquet) or repointing to the true upstream system

table calculation - verify DAX translation grain/filter-context against a known Tableau value

Tableau 'Measure Names/Measure Values' virtual pivot - no direct Power BI equivalent. Bind each resolved field in pivoted_field_ids directly as its own field on the target visual (e.g. one Y-axis field per measure on a clustered column chart), rather than trying to recreate a literal pivot column. (axis: columns, resolved fields: 2)

Resolved as measure [Rank over 3] = ROUND(RANKX(ALLSELECTED('Sales Planning'[Sales Person]), CALCULATE(SUM([Sales])), , DESC, DENSE)/3 - 0.6, 0)+1. Tableau INDEX() is positional/visual-sort dependent; approximated via RANKX by Sales desc and does NOT dynamically follow the 'Sort by' slicer. Report layer must verify banding against the intended visual sort order.

6 Tableau numeric range parameters modeled as Power BI What-If (GENERATESERIES) tables: Base Salary(0..200000/5000), Commission Rate(0..100/0.1), New Quota(0..2000000/25000), Churn Rate(0..1/0.01), New Business Growth(0..1/0.01), Top Customers(1..50/1). Min/max/step are reasonable assumptions (Tableau range params expose only a current value); each Value measure preserves the exact Tableau default via SELECTEDVALUE(<col>, <default>).

What-If calculated-table column bound with sourceColumn [Value] (bracketed) - confirmed as CalculatedTableColumn by TmdlSerializer and matches Power BI Desktop's own generated TMDL. Distinct from Field Parameter columns (Value1/Value2/Value3, no brackets). Correct data-refresh binding is only fully confirmable against a live model.

Tableau parameter 'Top Customers' (internal [Parameter 2], int range, default 15) is an ORPHAN - not referenced by any calculated field or worksheet (verified full-text). Modeled as a What-If table for fidelity/forward-looking use; safe to drop if unused by the report.

Tableau parameter 'Date for Calendar' (list 1/2, default 1) is an ORPHAN - not consumed by any calc/worksheet and not surfaced on the dashboard. Modeled as a disconnected 1-column slicer table for fidelity.

Tableau parameter 'Sort by' (internal [Parameter 1], list) modeled as a disconnected slicer feeding measure [Sort by field] (a sort key), NOT a Power BI Field Parameter - it swaps sort order, not a plotted measure/dimension. No Field Parameters were needed for this workbook (verified no parameter swaps a plotted field).

Capability-gap class 'no live free-text/date user-input parameter' (translation guide section 8) was evaluated and does NOT apply: all 8 Tableau parameters are numeric-range or discrete-list, fully representable as What-If sliders or disconnected slicers.

Tableau calculated field 'Quota' is a hardcoded constant (275000), not consumed by the single migrated worksheet (which uses the New Quota What-If param, default 500000). Preserved as constant measure [Quota] for fidelity.

Continuous numeric columns and currency measures modeled as double (matches Tableau 'real' and the reference migration). Power BI guidance often prefers fixed Decimal for currency to avoid binary-float rounding; revisit if exact cent-level equality is required.

All 11 tables are disconnected (0 relationships): the 3 Tableau data sources have no joins in the source, and What-If/slicer tables are intentionally disconnected (consumed via SELECTEDVALUE measures). Superstore Sales and WalMart Stores are secondary - not shown on the single migrated worksheet but retained for calc-field fidelity (Sales est, Annual sales growth, conversion timeline).

The 4 Quota-Attainment bucket colors on the Sales bars (Below 50% green, 50-75% blue, 75-100% orange, 100%+ teal) are implemented as rules-based conditional formatting on dataPoint.fill (Conditional.Cases, cascading '<' thresholds on [% of quota achieved]: 0.5/0.75/1.0 + Else). Structurally valid and mirrors a known-rendering pattern, but the discrete banding was not visually confirmed in Power BI Desktop in this environment (see report.desktop_verification).

Tableau 'Measure Names/Measure Values' pivot on the Compensation panel (parser UNRESOLVED:Multiple Values, UNRESOLVED:[:Measure Names]) has no PBI equivalent; resolved by binding the two underlying measures Commission (Variable) + Base Salary Value directly as the two stacked series of the Compensation barChart (Base stacks from 0 = gray, Commission = blue).

The 'New quota at $500K' reference line on the Sales chart and the 'OTE $159,000' line on the Compensation chart are static literal values (500000 and 159000=45000+0.228*500000). They do NOT move when the New Quota / Commission Rate / Base Salary What-If sliders change. PBI value-axis reference lines take a constant, not a measure.

The two right-side legends (Quota Attainment 4-color, Compensation 2-series) are built as multi-paragraph textboxes with U+25A0 colored squares, because the bar colors come from conditional formatting / per-series dataPoint colors rather than a single categorical legend field, so PBI's native legend cannot render them.

The 'Sort By' control is a disconnected slicer on 'Sort by'[Sort by] driving [Sort by field]; both charts sort by that measure Ascending. '% Quota Ascending/Descending' sort dynamically; 'Names' returns 0 for all rows and relies on the category (Sales Person) alphabetical tie-break to reproduce the default alphabetical order (works but is an implicit tie-break). The related [Rank over 3] measure remains a RANKX-by-Sales approximation of Tableau INDEX() and does not follow the Sort By selection (carried from semantic_build).

Tableau worksheet customized tooltip (Manager Name / % Quota Achieved / Total Sales / Compensation) is only partially reproduced via default tooltip fields ([% of quota achieved], [Total Compensation], [Sort by field]); the exact custom tooltip layout/labels are not reproduced.

The per-bar % labels use [% of quota achieved] with its model format string 0.0% (e.g. '81.0%'), whereas the Tableau reference shows integer percent (e.g. '81%'). Minor cosmetic difference; not changed to avoid a frozen-model edit.

Structural validation is clean (powerbi-report-author validate: 0 errors; only offline PBIR_SCHEMA_UNREACHABLE warning) and all 22 field references resolve against the model. Live Power BI Desktop screenshot verification was ATTEMPTED but blocked by environment: Store-install required PBI_DESKTOP_PATH; the TMDL-authored model shows 'calculated objects need to be manually refreshed' so the page never rendered and the bridge returned -32505 'Print metadata is not available'; concurrent report-build instances wedged the bridge. Recommend a manual Desktop open + Refresh + visual check of: (1) discrete band colors, (2) dynamic % labels, (3) What-If slicer defaults display, (4) two-chart row alignment, (5) Sort By behavior.
